compiled_state.title: Register session_log type + write-path rules (gate/lifecycle exempt, non-blocking links)
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
Register `session_log` as a first-class ticket type and enforce its write-path
rules: it can be created and edited/commented, but it is gate-exempt, cannot be
claimed or transitioned, and rejects blocking links while allowing non-blocking
ones.

## Why

This is the foundation the rest of the epic builds on. The type must exist and
behave correctly on the write path before performance isolation, the recent-logs
command, Jira exclusion, or capture tooling can be layered on.

## What

- Add `session_log` to the ticket-type enum in
  `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` and to `_TYPES` in
  `src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` (and its error message).
- Give it a hierarchy placement in `src/rebar/graph/_hierarchy.py` that keeps it
  out of blocking-link promotion (leaf / non-participating).
- Refuse `claim` and `transition` on a `session_log` with a clear message
  (lifecycle-exempt); keep `show` / `comment` / `edit` working as normal.
- In the link path, refuse `blocks` / `depends_on` for a `session_log` endpoint
  while allowing `relates_to` / `discovered_from`.
- Make `clarity_check` / `check_ac` / `quality_check` treat `session_log` as
  exempt (pass/skip) in `src/rebar/_engine_support/gates.py`.

## Scope

Write-path + type registration only. Read-path performance isolation, the recent
command, Jira exclusion, capture tooling, and docs are separate stories.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `rebar create session_log "..."` succeeds across schema + `_TYPES`.
- [ ] `claim` and `transition` on a `session_log` are refused with a clear error.
- [ ] `blocks` / `depends_on` to/from a `session_log` are refused; `relates_to` /
      `discovered_from` succeed.
- [ ] `clarity_check` / `check_ac` / `quality_check` return exempt/pass for a
      `session_log` regardless of body shape.
- [ ] `show`, `comment`, and `edit` operate normally on a `session_log`.
- [ ] Unit tests cover create, the refusals, the link rules, and gate exemption.

comments.body:
IMPLEMENTED (session-log loop). Registered the session_log ticket type + its write-path rules:
- Type registration: added session_log to the schema enum (src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json #/$defs/ticket_type) + _TYPES and the usage/error strings in src/rebar/_commands/composer.py.
- Hierarchy: session_log -> tier 0 (leaf) in graph/_hierarchy.py::_TYPE_TIER, AND refused as a blocking-link endpoint (see below), so it never participates in promotion.
- Lifecycle-exempt (no claim / no transition): guards in _commands/txn.py claim_core + transition_core (right after the reduce None-check, BEFORE the concurrency check → fires regardless of supplied current_status, covering library+CLI+MCP which all funnel through the locked cores). ALSO guarded the same-status no-op short-circuit in _commands/transition.py::transition_compute (it returns noop BEFORE reaching transition_core, so without this a  reported spurious success — caught by subagent review).
- Link rules: graph/_hierarchy.py::resolve_hierarchy_link refuses blocks/depends_on when either endpoint is a session_log (via the existing {error} channel → ValueError → RebarError), placed AFTER the non-blocking early-return so relates_to/duplicates/supersedes/discovered_from still link the exact pair.
- Gate-exempt: clarity_check/check_ac/quality_check in _engine_support/gates.py return verdict=pass (reason 'session_log is gate-exempt') regardless of body; return shapes satisfy clarity_result/gate_result schemas + the *_cli renderers (all counter keys present).
- show/comment/edit unchanged (work normally).
Tests: tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_session_log_type.py (10 cases via the rebar_repo fixture + library API → covers all three interfaces): create, invalid-type-still-rejected, claim refusal, transition refusal (incl. the no-op same-status case), blocking-link refusal both directions, non-blocking allowed (parametrized), gate exemption on an empty body, show/comment/edit. Subagent review clean after the noop fix. ruff check+format clean. Full offline suite green (2025). This is the foundation story; it UNBLOCKS the other 5 children (7657 graph/health isolation, e2e3 recent-logs cmd, 1368 jira exclusion, e7e4 capture helper, 8e10 docs).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
IMPLEMENTED (session-log loop). Registered the session_log ticket type + its write-path rules:
- Type registration: added session_log to the schema enum (src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json #/$defs/ticket_type) + _TYPES and the usage/error strings in src/rebar/_commands/composer.py.
- Hierarchy: session_log -> tier 0 (leaf) in graph/_hierarchy.py::_TYPE_TIER, AND refused as a blocking-link endpoint (see below), so it never participates in promotion.
- Lifecycle-exempt (no claim / no transition): guards in _commands/txn.py claim_core + transition_core (right after the reduce None-check, BEFORE the concurrency check -> fires regardless of supplied current_status, covering library+CLI+MCP which all funnel through the locked cores). ALSO guarded the same-status no-op short-circuit in _commands/transition.py::transition_compute (it returns noop BEFORE reaching transition_core, so without this a same-status transition reported spurious success -- caught by subagent review).
- Link rules: graph/_hierarchy.py::resolve_hierarchy_link refuses blocks/depends_on when either endpoint is a session_log (via the existing {error} channel -> ValueError -> RebarError), placed AFTER the non-blocking early-return so relates_to/duplicates/supersedes/discovered_from still link the exact pair.
- Gate-exempt: clarity_check/check_ac/quality_check in _engine_support/gates.py return verdict=pass (reason 'session_log is gate-exempt') regardless of body; return shapes satisfy clarity_result/gate_result schemas + the *_cli renderers (all counter keys present).
- show/comment/edit unchanged (work normally).
Tests: tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_session_log_type.py (10 cases via the rebar_repo fixture + library API -> covers all three interfaces): create, invalid-type-still-rejected, claim refusal, transition refusal (incl. the no-op same-status case), blocking-link refusal both directions, non-blocking allowed (parametrized), gate exemption on an empty body, show/comment/edit. Subagent review clean after the noop fix. ruff check+format clean. Full offline suite green (2026).
This is the foundation story; it UNBLOCKS the other 5 children (7657 graph/health isolation, e2e3 recent-logs cmd, 1368 jira exclusion, e7e4 capture helper, 8e10 docs).
